untranslatable_reason: Signal requires mean-reversion volatility model with long-term mean levels, event detection logic, and half-life decay calculations. These are composite statistical models not available as atomic indicators in the allowed set. Specifically missing: (1) long-term volatility mean calculation, (2) standard deviation bands around that mean, (3) event classification/detection mechanism, (4) mean-reversion speed parameter estimation, (5) Ornstein-Uhlenbeck or similar reversion half-life modeling. The thresholds [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] cannot be resolved without domain-specific volatility model implementation.
translation_notes: This signal represents a sophisticated volatility regime model that cannot be faithfully translated into basic technical indicators. It requires custom volatility forecasting logic beyond standard price/volume/momentum indicators. A backtest engine would need extended support for: (a) realized volatility calculations with configurable lookback windows, (b) event detection/classification, (c) mean-reversion halflife estimation, and (d) volatility forecasting models. The intent (trade mean-reversions in volatility after singular events) could be approximated using hvol_20 vs baseline, but would lose the core mean-reversion model sophistication.
